questions.q: Explain the importance of management in achieving organizational goals. Discuss the role of management in a modern business environment.
questions.answer: Management plays a crucial role in achieving organizational goals by ensuring effective and efficient use of resources. It involves planning, organizing, staffing, directing, and controlling various activities to achieve desired outcomes. In a modern business environment, management is essential for adapting to changing market conditions, technological advancements, and evolving customer needs. Effective management enables organizations to innovate, improve productivity, and maintain competitiveness.
marking_scheme: Importance of management in achieving organizational goals (1 mark)
Role of management in a modern business environment (2 marks)
Examples or illustrations to support the answer (2 marks)

questions.q: A company has a total output of 1000 units per day with 10 workers. If the daily wage per worker is ₹500, calculate the productivity per worker per day. Also, discuss the significance of productivity in the context of management.
questions.answer: Calculation: Productivity per worker per day = Total output / Number of workers = 1000 units / 10 workers = 100 units per worker per day. Productivity per worker per day in terms of wage = Total output / Total wage = 1000 units / (10 workers * ₹500) = 1000 units / ₹5000 = 0.2 units per ₹. The significance of productivity in management lies in its impact on efficiency, competitiveness, and overall performance of the organization.
marking_scheme: Calculation of productivity per worker per day (2 marks)
Calculation of productivity per worker per day in terms of wage (1 mark)
Significance of productivity in management (2 marks)

questions.q: Discuss the concept of management as a science, art, and profession. Explain the implications of each perspective on the practice of management.
questions.answer: Management can be viewed as a science, art, and profession. As a science, management involves the application of principles and theories to achieve predictable outcomes. As an art, management requires creativity, skill, and experience to deal with unique situations. As a profession, management demands a high level of expertise, ethical standards, and continuous learning. Each perspective has implications for the practice of management, such as the use of scientific methods, development of artistic skills, and adherence to professional norms.
marking_scheme: Management as a science (1.5 marks)
Management as an art (1.5 marks)
Management as a profession (1 mark)
Implications of each perspective on the practice of management (1 mark)

questions.q: A factory produces 500 units of a product per day with an average cost of ₹20 per unit. If the factory operates for 25 days in a month, calculate the total monthly production and the total monthly cost. Also, discuss the importance of cost control in management.
questions.answer: Calculation: Total monthly production = Daily production * Number of days = 500 units/day * 25 days = 12500 units. Total monthly cost = Daily production * Average cost per unit * Number of days = 500 units/day * ₹20/unit * 25 days = ₹250000. Cost control is essential in management as it helps organizations to optimize resource allocation, reduce waste, and improve profitability.
marking_scheme: Calculation of total monthly production (1 mark)
Calculation of total monthly cost (1 mark)
Importance of cost control in management (2 marks)
Examples or illustrations to support the answer (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the levels of management and their responsibilities. Discuss the importance of effective communication at each level of management.
questions.answer: The levels of management include top, middle, and lower levels. Top-level managers are responsible for strategic planning, middle-level managers for tactical planning, and lower-level managers for operational planning. Effective communication is crucial at each level to ensure that plans are implemented, goals are achieved, and problems are resolved. It involves conveying information, providing feedback, and facilitating collaboration among team members.
marking_scheme: Levels of management and their responsibilities (2 marks)
Importance of effective communication at each level (2 marks)
Examples or illustrations to support the answer (1 mark)

questions.q: A company has invested ₹100000 in a project with an expected return of 15% per annum. If the project is expected to operate for 5 years, calculate the total return on investment. Also, discuss the significance of return on investment (ROI) in management.
questions.answer: Calculation: Total return on investment = Principal amount * Rate of return * Time period = ₹100000 * 15% * 5 years = ₹100000 * 0.15 * 5 = ₹75000. The total return on investment over 5 years = ₹75000. The significance of ROI in management lies in its use as a performance metric to evaluate the profitability of investments, projects, and business units.
marking_scheme: Calculation of total return on investment (2 marks)
Significance of ROI in management (2 marks)
Examples or illustrations to support the answer (1 mark)
